RaceName: Killarney Park 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: No wins from 12 Flat starts and rated 48 in that sphere; 18l behind Words Unspoken at Limerick on hurdling debut.

RaceName: Killarney Park 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Ran poorly on sole Flat start and was pulled up on his first run over hurdles at Limerick, jumping lacking fluency; unlikely.

RaceName: Killarney Park 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Best of eight Flat runs came in a 1m3f handicap here in May (good) when a close third; below that level twice since; rated 62 on Flat, so may get involved on hurdling/stable debut.

RaceName: Killarney Park 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Placed twice in Roscommon maidens last year and again twice on AW earlier this year; claimed for 5,000euros after disappointing in a Ballinrobe claimer in May; not too bad in a Down Royal maiden last time; rated 63 on Flat and not discounted.

RaceName: Killarney Park 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Beat very few rivals in three Flat maidens last year; modest sixth of 14 on Flat handicap debut at Gowran last time; hails from a yard that means he can't be ruled out despite a Flat rating of 45; hood goes on for hurdling debut

RaceName: Killarney Park 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Beaten out of sight in sole Flat maiden; didn't look a natural jumper when unseating at the second on hurdling debut at Limerick (150-1).

RaceName: Killarney Park 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Surprise winner (20-1) on his seventh Flat start at Bellewstown last time (1m4f, good); that brings him up to a rating of 63, which still leaves him with a bit to find with a few of these.

RaceName: Killarney Park 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: One of only three in the field with hurdling experience and he has finished well ahead of the other two; the 85,000euros yearling lost third late on at Limerick (2m1f, good), but it was a positive first-ever run and there is likely improvement to come.

RaceName: Killarney Park 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: 110,000euros yearling; two good placed efforts in Flat maidens this season; her Flat rating of 74 is one of the best in this field for her hurdling debut.

RaceName: Killarney Park 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Basement rating of 37 on Flat following nine unplaced runs; others preferred.

RaceName: Killarney Park 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Couple of okay AW runs over the winter before making a winning turf/handicap debut in May (1m3f, yielding to soft), runner-up has won since; slight concern how poor she was at Down Royal when last seen (beaten 21l); however, her Flat rating of 74 gives her a big shout here on hurdling bow.

RaceName: Killarney Park 3-Y-O Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Rated 51 on the Flat after seven runs with little success; mid-field here over 1m4f on Monday; Misty Cove is the more appealing of yard's two runners.

RaceName: Killarney Grand Mares Maiden Hurdle
OwnerName: John J Flynn, Pierce Flynn, Conor Lannen
Spotlight: Wexford bumper winner last summer and easily pick of three attempts hurdling when second to an odds-on shot in 2m Listowel mares maiden last month (yielding); later in month just okay effort in Leopardstown amateur flat maiden on fast ground; probably prefers easier ground and could struggle to confirm Listowel form with Tumbling In on these terms.

RaceName: Killarney Grand Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Off the mark in bumpers last month at Sligo (2m2f, good), having previously only just been denied in a 3-finisher 3m Kilbeggan novice hurdle; yard in flying form this week, one to consider although this looks a tougher test.

RaceName: Killarney Grand Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Debut Galway festival bumper winner last season before running solid races for third in three subsequent Listed bumpers without showing much progress; last month's hurdles debut in 2m6f Punchestown mares maiden was tame; surely capable of better, although stamina a possible issue.

RaceName: Killarney Grand Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Huge odds and well beaten in all five starts; hard to make a case for on form.

RaceName: Killarney Grand Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Nice bit of improvement on third maiden hurdle outing, left in second at the last to chase home an odds-on shot over 2m4f at Limerick in late May (yielding); definite contender if seeing out this longer trip.

RaceName: Killarney Grand Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Not much promise in three point-to-to-points and well held on last month's bumper debut; easily passed over now switched to hurdles.

RaceName: Killarney Grand Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Moderate bumper form and two maiden hurdle runs no better.

RaceName: Killarney Grand Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Limited level of ability hurdling in 2024 and more recent point-to-point form doesn't inspire confidence.

RaceName: Killarney Grand Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Slowly improving, fair fifth over C&D in May and later in month stayed on late to chase home a hot favourite at Tramore; tongue-tie now added to hood; more likely needed to play a major role.

RaceName: Killarney Grand Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Huge price when showing mild promise with a recent fourth placing at Downpatrick but unable to build on that at Limerick since; upped in trip and lots more needed.

RaceName: Killarney Grand Mares Maiden Hurdle
OwnerName: Mrs Paul Shanahan & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Sister to 134-rated hurdler Dream On Baby showed potential in two bumpers last season and much better effort on hurdles debut when returning last month, effort just flattening out from last when third in 2m Listowel mares maiden; 1.25l to find with Fraher Field, but now gets 7lb from that rival and bred to relish this longer trip.

RaceName: Killarney Grand Mares Maiden Hurdle
HorseName: Uno Me I Like My T
Spotlight: Poor overall form including in three maiden hurdles; tongue-tie tried.

RaceName: Killarney Grand Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Tailed off in both bumpers in the spring; 4yo can only be watched now switched to hurdles.

RaceName: Killarney Grand Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Order Of St George filly is first foal of a modest half-sister to winners Early Morning Rain (bumper and 2m/2m1f hurdle), Rosa Damascena (1m2f) and Matavia Bay (1m2f); debutante best watched.

RaceName: Killarney Grand Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Soundly beaten in maidens at Kilbeggan and Bellewstown; best watched for now.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB)
Spotlight: Made a winning debut at Nottingham (6f, good) in May but found the move up to Group class a step too far when down the field in the Albany Stakes at Royal Ascot (6f, good to firm) last month; she should find this company more to her liking on first AW start and should run well.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB)
Spotlight: Slow to start when fifth of six on her debut at Wetherby (5.5f, good to firm; second favourite) last month; will be wiser on this AW debut and likely to show more.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB)
Spotlight: 28-1 when third of five on her debut at Newbury (7f, good to firm) last month; steps back in trip on her AW debut; should run well.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB)
Spotlight: 6-1, made up ground from the rear to finish fourth of ten on her debut at Newbury (6f, good to firm) last month; this looks easier for AW debut.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB)
Spotlight: 18,000gns foals; 29,000gns yearlings; fourth foal; by Mohaather; half-sister to 7f AW/7.4f turf 2yo winner Liberty Bird (RPR 72); dam German 7f Listed winner (95), out of maiden half-sister to 7f 2yo winner Screen Star (dam of Cheveley Park winner Lumiere); yard is having plenty of juvenile winners and market should be best guide on this debut.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB)
Spotlight: 20,000gns breeze-up buy; well beaten in a couple of maidens; more needed on AW debut.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB)
Spotlight: 20,000gns yearling; third foal; by Harry Angel; half-sister to winners Havana Blast (5f AW 2yo; RPR 90) and Argy Bhaji (5f; 75); dam 5f winner (67), closely related to 5f winner Sitar, out of unraced half-sister to Group 1 winners Airwave (Cheveley Park) and Jwala (Nunthorpe); plenty of speed in her pedigree; wears tongue-tie on debut; betting watch advised.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB)
Spotlight: 250-1 when tailed off on her Windsor debut (6f, good to soft) last month.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Needed the experience in one run last winter and fared much better on her Tapeta comeback, when easily beating a subsequent winner in a weak maiden at Wolverhampton (upped to 9.5f; stamina on the dam's side); should have more to offer under the penalty.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Shaped nicely at a big price on her soft-ground debut early last month and ran to a similar level last time (good; both over this trip); enters calculations on either effort.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Dam was a limited maiden and this filly has followed suit thus far; lost a shoe on her handicap debut last time but, while this race is less competitive, she's had plenty of chances now.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 25,000euros yearling whose dam won on the AW at around 1m in France; hood goes on for her debut and the market will be the best guide for a yard that's enjoying a decent summer.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (Horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Started a single-figure price for the Wolverhampton maiden won by Hauteluce late last month but blew the start and was never a factor; blinkers are tried after that.

RaceName: Lee Strand Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three hurdle wins in second half of 2024 haven't been added to, including in a stint chasing; most recently creditable fourth in 2m5f Kilbeggan handicap in May; back hurdling and ideally wants softer ground.

RaceName: Lee Strand Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Four-time winner over hurdles in Britain with Jamie Snowden at up to 2m5f, also a dual winner over fences; well beaten in Pertemps Final and pulled up (lost right shoe) at Haydock in April final start before bought for 18,000GBP following month; interesting to see if new yard can spark a revival, ground to suit.

RaceName: Lee Strand Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Capable handicap hurdler back in the day (won here in 2022 over 2m4f); series of absences in recent years, most recently good run in the circumstances when hampered by loose horse in finishing fourth in 2m4f Ballinrobe handicap chase in May; first run over hurdles in three years (11lb lower here).

RaceName: Lee Strand Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: In fine form, winning over 2m4f at Punchestown festival and good efforts off much higher marks since, chasing home an 8l winner over this trip at Ballinrobe and not quite getting home over 3m1f when third at Kilbeggan last month; trip/ground okay and should go well again.

RaceName: Lee Strand Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three-time winner for Mark Fahey in 2023, including over this trip at Galway off today's mark; some lengthy absences since, most recently encouraging comeback run at Ballinrobe in late May when staying-on third (Jacovec Cavern second) over this trip (good) in first-time cheekpieces; entitled to improve for that and has 4l to find with Jacovec Cavern.

RaceName: Lee Strand Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Two-time winner over hurdles, including 2m4f novice here in 2024; not so effective a chaser, most recently dropping away tamely after forcing much of pace in 2m5f Kilbeggan handicap in May; blinkers now replace cheekpieces on hurdles return with a bit to prove.

RaceName: Lee Strand Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Both hurdle wins came at Cork on good ground last year; 0-4 chasing this year, just headed when unseating two out at Limerick last time; reverts to hurdles with trip/ground to suit.

RaceName: Lee Strand Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Both hurdle wins have been at around 2m on soft ground; tame enough Listowel effort last month at similar trip, now takes a marked step up in distance and others look more likely.

RaceName: Lee Strand Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual winner over both hurdles and fences on decent ground; two recent runs leave her with a fair bit to find and cheekpieces now tried.

RaceName: Lee Strand Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual chase winner who is rated lower over hurdles, making all over 2m7f at Clonmel in April and fair fourth here following month (2m4f); return to longer trip to suit and yard going well.

RaceName: Lee Strand Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Two hurdle wins came over shorter trips and off the mark chasing in 2m5f Kilbeggan handicap in May, following that up with good third in competitive 2m4f handicap at same track; clearly in good order and could go well here off 8lb lower hurdles mark.

RaceName: Lee Strand Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Limerick maiden winner last year (2m1f, good) and finished good third in the Lartigue at Listowel; easily best run since and just beginning his challenge (going well) when unseating two out here in May in race won by Burru over 2m4f; poor Listowel run later in month and has had a break since (upped in trip).

RaceName: Lee Strand Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Sligo maiden hurdle winner last year (2m2f, good) but needs to improve a fair bit on both recent handicap efforts; steps up in trip; due to run here yesterday.

RaceName: Lee Strand Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: J T O'Connor & M P Gleeson & P M Fortune & A McGonigle
Spotlight: Winner of a 2m2f Cork novice event on good ground in May, not so good in Wexford conditions event later same month; seemingly has a bit to do back in handicap company off this sort of mark.

RaceName: Lee Strand Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Got up late to win a 2m Kilbeggan maiden hurdle in September 2024 (good-to-yielding) but hasn't been seen since; very much an unknown quantity on handicap debut after such an absence and market probably best guide.

RaceName: Lee Strand Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: First reserve; three hurdle wins have been at Limerick but also won 2m5f handicap chase here last summer; absent since pulled up over fences at Limerick's Christmas meeting so may need this.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Has nine career wins, the most recent at Lingfield (7f, AW) in February off 2lb lower; should be better for his fourth of eight at Lingfield (6f, soft) last month and has won off 8lb higher when in his prime; not out of this.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Useful 3yo for Tom Clover but has gone backwards since; sold for 2,000gns to join current yard this month; risks involved in yet another change of headgear on stable debut.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Scored off 2lb lower at Lingfield (6f, AW) in February and ran his best race since when fourth of seven at Ffos Las (6f, good) last month; not out of this.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Has done all of his racing on turf bar one run at Newcastle in 2024; made a most encouraging return from an eight-month break when runner-up at Pontefract (6f, good) last month; should go close if able to reproduce that effort back on AW.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Has an inconsistent profile but scored off just 1lb lower at Newmarket (6f, good) in May; cannot be ruled out.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Has been kept busy and scored for the second time this year when successful over C&D last month off 6lb lower; not disgraced in better company at Epsom eight days ago but may need to come back down the weights.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
OwnerName: P R Hedger & P C F Racing Ltd
Spotlight: Scored over C&D in December off 2lb lower and landed his tenth career win at Bath (5f, good) in April; last of eight though over C&D when last seen and vulnerable to younger rivals.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Got off the mark at Windsor (6f, good) in May and race may have come too soon when running flat three days later; still unexposed and may be worth another chance after a short break.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Has shown promise in her three novices and may not have got home over 7f last time; sports first-time tongue-tie on handicap debut and has scope to show improvement; claims.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Showed improved form when getting off the mark at Southwell (6f, AW) in April; never figured when fifth of nine at Leicester (6f, good) last month; not one to give up on.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up for the second time at Leicester (5f, good) last month and should be suited by stepping back up in trip; should run well.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Prominent racer; made all to win over 6f at Lingfield in January; similar form when third over 6f there the following month; gelded since disappointing over 5f in March; needs to be back to his best here.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Handicap
Spotlight: Sole success came at Bath (5f, heavy) last October; shapes as though this return to 6f will suit but this is tough on AW debut.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-brother to the yard's dual 6f 2yo winner Egoli; started among the favourites for a Salisbury novice that's produced winners a month ago; blew the start and was never involved that day but is entitled to improve.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: First foal of an unraced sister to useful Irish sprinter Son Of Rest; trainer's strike-rate with 2yos is down a touch this season and the market will guide.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Boasts a decent pedigree but started a big price on his C&D debut three weeks ago, when well held having been inclined to pull hard; will need to improve a deal on that.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Yard won this with a debutant in 2023; this 325,000euros Irish breeze-up 2yo, who's a half-brother to two Irish winners, holds a Gimcrack (Group 2) entry and is a likely looking newcomer.

RaceName: EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Big price and badly needed the experience on his recent Epsom debut, blowing the start and finishing a remote last of eight; needs a lot more, dropping back from 7f.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Won a 6f maiden at Brighton (good to firm) on debut in May; well-beaten last at Kempton (7f) 18 days ago and surprising if he could overcome his penalty in this company.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Second foal; half-brother to 1m 2yo/9.4f AW winner Crockham Heath (RPR 85); dam French Listed-placed 1m winner (85), half-sister to winners All Rumours (1m2f Listed) and Trachonitus (1m 2yo); Andrew Balding has won this with subsequent Dewhurst winners Gewan (on debut) and Chaldean (second time out) in recent seasons; of obvious interest.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Clear signs of ability in novice events here (6.5f, good to soft) and Lingfield (7f, AW) this summer; that form will likely need bettering if he is to get off the mark in this field; Richard Hannon runs three here.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Runner-up in novice events at Nottingham (6f, good) and Sandown (7f, good) either side of his midfield finish in the Coventry Stakes at Royal Ascot; sets the standard and should win races but this could be warm.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 27,000euros foal, 28,000euros yearling; third foal; half-brother to winners Akvarel (Polish 5f-1m including 2yo) and Argon Flash (Italian 7f/7.5f); dam made frame 10.5f-12.5f in France, half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Coral Beach; stable house an exciting 2yo prospect, who made a winning debut, so worth a market check.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Seventh foal; brother to 6f/7f 2yo winner Shadow Of Light (including 2yo Group 1; RPR 119), closely related to 5.5f-7f winner Earthlight (including 2yo Group 1; 119), half-brother to two winners including Wild Desert (7f 2yo; 105); dam Group-placed 7f 2yo winner (109); looks the part on paper and stable's 2yos operating at a fine strike-rate.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Market mover prior to his debut at Thirsk (7f, good) in May, a race where he shaped nicely; he will need a good step forward to come out on top here though.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Teme Valley, C Rosier & Mrs J Rosier
Spotlight: 30,000euros yearling, 80,000euros breeze-up 2yo; third foal; dam Listed-placed 7f 2yo winner (RPR 99), half-sister to winners Ocean Tempest (1m Listed) and Mohawk Ridge (1m-2m); one to note for market confidence.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 32,000gns yearling; fourth foal; closely related to Swedish 1m/8.6f winner Showgal, half-brother to winners Orchard Keeper (1m2f/1m6f; RPR 99) and The Hrse Horse (Qatari 7f); dam unraced half-sister to Group 3 winners Best Name (1m2f) and February Sun (1m3f); stable in fine form but others have more appealing pedigrees.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Met all sorts of trouble when sixth on his debut at Nottingham (6f, good; Ciilician second); hot favourite for a 6f Kempton maiden 18 days ago on the strength of it but he could only manage fourth; still has time on his side but Oisin Murphy rides stablemate Arthurian.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Second run was more promising than his debut but it would still be surprising if there weren't a few stronger in today's field; stable run three in the race.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 20,000gns yearling; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to smart winners Quick Wit (7f-1m2f) and Gallagher (6f 2yo/7f), out of Listed-placed 5f/6f 2yo winning half-sister to Gimcrack winner Bannister; stable may hold stronger claims elsewhere.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 85,000euros yearling; fourth foal; brother to 7f 2yo winner Major Neigh Sayer (RPR 86); dam placed 1m2f-1m4f (79), half-sister to 6f/7f 2yo Group 1 winner Dabirsim; should have a future but this looks a tough starting point.

RaceName: Ire-Incentive, It Pays To Buy Irish EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 400,000gns yearling; third foal; brother to Listed-placed 7f 2yo winner Rochfortbridge (RPR 91), half-brother to 7f AW winner Mapledurham (72); dam unraced half-sister to US 7f Grade 1 winner First Defence, out of US 7f Grade 1 winner; good pedigree and one to note for market confidence.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Daily Money Back Meeting Handicap Chase
Spotlight: 0-7 since winning on chase debut early last year but has faced some tough tasks in competitive handicaps, notably good third over 2m1f at Ascot in November; not much luck in running when midfield in Grand Annual in March and has had a break since; drops in class here, so has to be respected.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Daily Money Back Meeting Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Two chase wins came in 2024 but some good runs in competitive handicaps last season, notably Grand Annual fifth and decent fourth in Red Rum at Aintree; down in grade with conditions to suit, so big player.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Daily Money Back Meeting Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Much better chaser than hurdler, three wins include handicaps at Punchestown and Cork in 2024; long losing run since, but has shown himself in good heart on the Flat of late.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Daily Money Back Meeting Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Seven career wins capped by 2m1f Listed handicap chase win at Fairyhouse last November; recent Kilbeggan runs over longer trips well below best, but yard has returned to form so could improve for this drop in trip, although yard have a more obvious contender in Gaelic Arc.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Daily Money Back Meeting Handicap Chase
OwnerName: Mark Devlin & Philip Smith & Nigel O'Hare & P J Rothwell
Spotlight: Veteran's most recent win came in this race last year off 2lb lower; rediscovered form in the spring in 2m4f veterans' handicaps, finishing fine third at Haydock and good second (having hit last) at Ballinrobe; has to be considered.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Daily Money Back Meeting Handicap Chase
OwnerName: Mark R D Fleming / T Westropp Bennett
Spotlight: Won this in 2024 off 1lb higher mark and timely return to form last month under forcing tactics, winning over 2m at Punchestown and only denied by a neck off 5lb higher over 2m4f at Kilbeggan; up another 3lb but likely big player again.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Daily Money Back Meeting Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Hurdles winner has been placed only twice in 12 chase starts, notably beaten a neck in 2m2f Limerick novice at Christmas on testing ground; pulled up in first-time cheekpieces (now discarded) on Kilbeggan comeback in late May and questions to answer now, especially on this quicker ground.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Daily Money Back Meeting Handicap Chase
Spotlight: Bumper and hurdles winner for Willie Mullins in 2023-24; didn't add to her tally for Paul Kiely last year, but back to form on both recent starts for current yard in 2m handicap chases on good ground, winning at Tramore and chasing home clear winner Mic Drop off 6lb higher at Wexford; more needed to reverse that form.

RaceName: BOYLE Sports Daily Money Back Meeting Handicap Chase
Spotlight: In the form of his life and seeking a hat-trick, following wins at Limerick and when beating Femme Magnifique decisively at Wexford last month (good); 2lb wrong here, but talented rider's claim offsets that; big player again, although may need to jump better now raised in class.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Last of eight on belated debut at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) three weeks ago and needs to step up considerably on AW debut up in trip.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed only modest form in a couple of 1m novices on turf last month; steps back in trip on AW debut and improvement required.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 85,000gns yearling; fifth foal; by Lope Y Fernandez; closely related to 6f/7f winner Dubai Poet (including Australian Listed; RPR 110); dam 6f winner (90), closely related to 7f Group 3 winner Air Chief Marshall, half-sister to 1m6f Group 1 winner Flag Of Honour; makes debut and any market move should be noted.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Gelded prior to his second run when an encouraging third of nine at Kempton (7f, AW) 15 days ago; should be spot on and is one to keep on side.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Last of six in a Brighton maiden (6f, good to firm) on debut last month; has been gelded and wears first-time hood stepping up in trip on this AW debut; more required.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has shown only modest form so far, including when ninth of 12 over C&D last month; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 65,000euros foal; sixth foal; by Lucky Vega; half-brother to winners Secret Time (French/US Group-placed 7f; RPR 99) and Sabiano (French/German 9.5f-1m3f); dam 6f 2yo winner (86), sister to Group-placed 7f 2yo winner Caprella, out of 5f/6f winner; sports tongue-tie on debut and best watched for now.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has finished runner-up three times, latest at Kempton (7f) 15 days ago; she sets the benchmark here and wears first-time cheekpieces; looks sure to go close.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed promise in a couple of maidens on turf last autumn; this is her first run since and the market will be best guide on this AW debut.

RaceName: When You Wish Upon A Star Race Handicap
Spotlight: Three wins over hurdles since last seen on the Flat, going almost two stone up the weights; withdrawn on account of unsuitable (good to firm) ground last summer, which would be a concern, but she's much better treated on the Flat now.

RaceName: When You Wish Upon A Star Race Handicap
Spotlight: Adaptable regarding ground and tactics; this looks less competitive than the Ffos Las 0-65 in which he finished second last time (hampered; two of these behind) and his claims are obvious.

RaceName: When You Wish Upon A Star Race Handicap
Spotlight: Better expected last time, after two poor efforts and back to the visor from cheekpieces, but ran below market expectations in finishing behind Merrijig; 2lb better off today but has work to do if he's to turn things round.

RaceName: When You Wish Upon A Star Race Handicap
OwnerName: Case Racing Partnership & Anita J Lush
Spotlight: Made all off 3lb higher here (1m6f) last July; not in the same form this season, including in that same race a fortnight ago, when a long way off the front pair; needs more.

RaceName: When You Wish Upon A Star Race Handicap
Spotlight: String of poor efforts to date, latterly when a long way behind Merrijig and Simiyann at Ffos Las late last month; would be a shock winner.

RaceName: Boost Tokens Now Live At BetVictor EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 70,000euros foal, 145,000gns yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to winners Utxa (French 1m1f 2yo/Czech 1m3f) and Sopran Beethoven (Italian 6f); dam once-raced close relative of 6f/7f Listed winner Rose Bonheur, out of 1m Group 3 winner; William Buick booked; one to consider.

RaceName: Boost Tokens Now Live At BetVictor EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Smart pedigree; made a promising debut when fourth of ten in a fillies' maiden at Yarmouth (6f, good to firm) four weeks ago; should do much better; respected.

RaceName: Boost Tokens Now Live At BetVictor EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £52,000 yearling, 90,000euros breeze-up 2yo; seventh foal; half-sister to winners Manhattan Jungle (French 5f 2yo/US 6.5f Listed; RPR 95), She's A Warrior (1m; 89), Gregory Ligh (Italian 8.7f) and Raccontero Di Te (prolific Italian 5f/6f); dam 1m AW 2yo winner (59); hooded for debut; another to monitor closely in the betting.

RaceName: Boost Tokens Now Live At BetVictor EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Third foal; half-sister to winners Anna Swan (Listed-placed 7f 2yo; RPR 90) and Dark Shore (Qatari 1m2f); dam Group-placed 7f-1m2f winner (including 2yo; 106), sister to German/UAE 1m6f Group 3 winner Ispolini; bred to need more of a test but she represents powerful connections and a market check is advised.

RaceName: Boost Tokens Now Live At BetVictor EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 66-1 for her debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last month when showing some ability but ultimately finishing well beaten; likely  a longer-term prospect.

RaceName: Boost Tokens Now Live At BetVictor EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-sister to connections' useful Palmar Bay; encouraging start to her career when fourth of 13 over C&D (good to firm) a fortnight ago and she is open to improvement; high on the list.

RaceName: Boost Tokens Now Live At BetVictor EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Second foal; dam Group-placed 6f/7f 2yo winner (including AW; RPR 100), closely related to 6f/7f winner William Dewhirst, half-sister to Italian Listed-placed 7.5f/1m1f winner Crook; enough paper appeal to warrant a market check.

RaceName: Boost Tokens Now Live At BetVictor EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Slowly away and never in the hunt at Windsor (6f, good to soft) on last month's debut; big leap forward needed.

RaceName: Boost Tokens Now Live At BetVictor EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 175,000gns yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to winners Manatee Mehmas (5f; RPR 95) and Tiva (5f; 86); dam placed 6f (including 2yo; 71), closely related to 6f Group 2 winner Tasleet and Group-placed 6f 2yo winner Aspect Island; yard seeking first 2yo winner of the year but she's of interest on paper.

RaceName: Boost Tokens Now Live At BetVictor EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Fifth foal; dam 6f/7f 2yo winner (including Group 3; RPR 102), half-sister to Group-placed 5f-7.4f winner Motafaawit, out of 6f 2yo winning half-sister to 7f Listed winner Mankib; another well-bred newcomer who requires a market check.

RaceName: Boost Tokens Now Live At BetVictor EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 7,000gns foal, 32,000gns yearling; first foal; dam unraced sister to winners Monaasib (Group-placed 6f/6.4f 2yo) and Just A Girl (7.6f 2yo), half-sister to 5f Listed winner Tarboosh, out of 6f Listed winner; likely best watched on debut.

RaceName: Boost Tokens Now Live At BetVictor EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 92,000gns foal, 100,000gns yearling; first foal of an unraced half-sister to winners Galmarley (useful 1m5f) and Gallifrey (1m 2yo/12.4f), out of 1m Listed winner from family of Derby runner-up Walk In The Park; may need more of a test in time.

RaceName: Boost Tokens Now Live At BetVictor EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Third foal; dam a maiden (RPR 62) half-sister to a German Listed winner;  perhaps one for the longer term.

RaceName: Lee Strand Novice Chase
Spotlight: Improved form lately in easily winning a Downpatrick maiden hurdle before adding a Tramore beginners' chase (2m, good), keeping on well; his chase win was the least impressive of the three last-time winners, but not ruled out given his good form.

RaceName: Lee Strand Novice Chase
Spotlight: 1-5 over hurdles and rated 121; unseated at the third on chase debut at Leopardstown in March; made amends when comfortably winning at Ballinrobe last time (2m1f, good); that run gives him a shot here.

RaceName: Lee Strand Novice Chase
Spotlight: Top-rated of these over hurdles (133), winning four from ten; trounced his four rivals on chasing debut at Limerick in May (2m4f, good to yielding), jumping fluently and travelling strongly; that was a very promising first run over fences and he can make it back-to-back wins despite dropping back in trip.

RaceName: Lee Strand Novice Chase
Spotlight: Won back-to-back handicaps at Cork (2m1f, good) and Tipperary (2m, good to yielding) in August/October; hasn't hit the same heights in three runs since, beaten 16.5l at this track last time (2m1f, good to yielding); unplaced in three points so this switch to chasing may not bring the required improvement.

RaceName: Lee Strand Novice Chase
Spotlight: Won back-to-back hurdle races in August/September on good ground; creditable fourth at this track in May (2m1f, good) but below that at Listowel last time; makes chasing debut, but has a lot to find with a few of these.

RaceName: Lee Strand Novice Chase
Spotlight: Won his second hurdles start at Limerick in November (2m, heavy) and placed a couple of times since then; beaten 23l off 105 at Roscommon last time on chase debut (2m1f, yielding to soft); others make more appeal.

RaceName: Lee Strand Novice Chase
Spotlight: 1-11 over hurdles having won a Downpatrick handicap off 86 in August (2m2f, good); made a bad mistake when tailed off on chasing bow at Wexford last time (2m4f, good); others preferred.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins came at this track at around 1m in autumn 2024; placed a couple of times in March on AW but below that form on turf twice since; may do better back on AW.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Secured her first AW win at Southwell (7f) in April; runner-up back there (7f) 12 days ago; needs things to drop right given hold-up tactics are employed; holds decent claims if they do.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Looked one to keep on the right side of when sweeping through late to win at Lingfield last summer (6f, AW); generally held her form since and she is now 2lb below her winning mark; she's a key player, albeit she could do with them going hard early.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Fairly consistent sort; ran up to her best when runner-up here (6f) 11 days ago; first-time hood replaces visor and good chance that stepping back up in trip can eke out further improvement.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Got off the mark in a Southwell maiden (6f, AW) in April but disappointing in a couple of turf handicaps since; needs to bounce back to her best returning to AW and stepping back up in trip.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins have come over 6f on Newcastle's AW; not in the same form on turf since; steps up in trip back on AW and cannot be discounted.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Got off the mark at Kempton (7f, AW) in May off 3lb lower; has run respectably since and the return to her winning distance is a plus.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Scored at Kempton (1m, AW) in January and has been consistent since but is 6lb higher than her winning mark so needs more.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Hasn't gone on since winning her first two starts in 2024; yet to win a handicap and despite a declining mark others are preferred.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Showed much improved form when making a winning handicap debut at Kempton (7f, AW) last month despite not getting a clear run; 4lb higher but there should be more to come from her and she holds solid claims.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: 0-12 and and already with her fourth trainer, she nevertheless ran her best race to date when runner-up here (6f) ten days ago; steps back up in trip and shouldn't be far away.

RaceName: XMA Wish Race "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Who Have Not Won More Than One Race)
Spotlight: Podium finishes on all three 2yo novice starts for Mark Rimell, almost holding on at Redcar (6f, good; bumped late); yet to scale those heights in handicaps this term despite starting off on a tolerable mark, traffic not costing him a win latest; hopes rest with change of scenery since.

RaceName: XMA Wish Race "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Who Have Not Won More Than One Race)
Spotlight: Neck second at Newcastle (5f, AW) last time rates a clear career best, and a 3lb rise doesn't look prohibitive; yet to see out a galloping 6f completely convincingly, albeit not having yet tried to on going as quick as today's.

RaceName: XMA Wish Race "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Who Have Not Won More Than One Race)
Spotlight: A failure under patient tactics aside, she proved consistent last autumn and winter; timely return to 6f judged on recent keeping-on Tapeta runs, but she's still possibly high in the weights despite 1lb more off.

RaceName: XMA Wish Race "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Who Have Not Won More Than One Race)
Spotlight: Stayed on as strongly as any at Windsor latest (6f, good to firm) after a slow start; more galloping test at the trip is a likely plus, and just a case of whether he helps himself sufficiently; eased 1lb.

RaceName: XMA Wish Race "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Who Have Not Won More Than One Race)
Spotlight: Unimproved for the addition of cheekpieces (retained here nonetheless) at Ripon in May, and wind surgery resorted to since; effectiveness over a galloping 6f yet to be tested, but 2lb lower mark looks competitive judged against the RPR of last autumn's Leicester debut (5f).

RaceName: XMA Wish Race "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Who Have Not Won More Than One Race)
Spotlight: Defied usual hard-pulling and hanging traits to win at Redcar in May (7f, good to firm), but held off mid-60s marks since, already headed when stumbling latest; replication of previous novice/maiden 6f form won't do.

RaceName: XMA Wish Race "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Who Have Not Won More Than One Race)
Spotlight: Won going away at Newcastle in March (6f, AW) for a perfect start to life in handicaps; refusal to settle and (latest) traffic issues offer excuses since, but less margin for error now stepped up beyond 0-60 class.

RaceName: Apple Print & Creative Rose Bowl Stakes (Listed Race)
OwnerName: A Bit On The Side & Partner
Spotlight: Won the Brocklesby in March; ran well at Royal Ascot (sixth of 25) and showed better form when second of eight in a Listed race at Sandown (5f, good; Ronson and Underdog in behind) two weeks ago; return to 6f should suit; respected.

RaceName: Apple Print & Creative Rose Bowl Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: All runs at 5f, winning an AW novice in May before a creditable eighth in the Group 2 Norfolk Stakes at the Royal meeting; 6f should suit; not fully exposed.

RaceName: Apple Print & Creative Rose Bowl Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Well found in the market prior to his winning debut at Thirsk (5f, good to firm) in May; played up in the stalls before being withdrawn at Beverley four weeks later; easily justified 2-9 favouritism back at Thirsk (6f, good to firm) 16 days ago and he's a promising juvenile; well worth his place in this higher grade.

RaceName: Apple Print & Creative Rose Bowl Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Won well over C&D (good) on his debut in May; 25-1 and well beaten in the Coventry Stakes last month; still has time to do better but others bring more compelling credentials.

RaceName: Apple Print & Creative Rose Bowl Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: 3l behind God Given Talent over C&D on debut; 200-1 when appearing to excel himself in the Coventry Stakes, beaten just over 4l into tenth; remains capable of better.

RaceName: Apple Print & Creative Rose Bowl Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: 850,000gns yearling; unextended when landing odds of 2-9 in a four-runner maiden at Yarmouth (6f, good to firm) two weeks ago; this is a much sterner test of his credentials but no surprise to see him in the thick of it.

RaceName: Apple Print & Creative Rose Bowl Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Impressed when winning a maiden at Hamilton (5f, good to firm) in May; confirmed he was a promising juvenile by finishing fifth in the Windsor Castle at Royal Ascot (6f, good to firm), doing best of those racing on the unfavoured far side; capable of better still and he's a major player.

RaceName: Apple Print & Creative Rose Bowl Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: 120,000euros yearling; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Online Alexander (useful 5f including 2yo) and Bnedel (1m 2yo/10.4f), out of 7f winning half-sister to 6f Group 1 winner Red Clubs; stiff task on debut; stable also run Agamemnon.

RaceName: Apple Print & Creative Rose Bowl Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Ready win at Ripon (5f, good) on debut; finished fastest when fourth of 21 in the Norfolk Stakes four weeks ago; 6f looks sure to suit and he retains considerable potential.

RaceName: Apple Print & Creative Rose Bowl Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Won a 5f novice at Sandown on debut; he has finished behind reopposing rivals in two runs since and others look more persuasive.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div I)
Spotlight: Doubled his tally over fences when winning this race last year off 8lb lower (yielding to soft) under O'Keeffe; has run just once since then, on seasonal return at Punchestown when an okay eighth of 18 in a 2m handicap hurdle (good to yielding); cheekpieces go on now and he has won on good; interesting.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div I)
Spotlight: Modest strike-rate of 3-40 over fences, although one of his wins came here; latest success was in August 2024 at Kilbeggan; showed more promise returned to there last time (2m5f, yielding) when a distant third of 11; he's now 19lb below his last winning mark, but must build on his last run.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div I)
Spotlight: Shed his maiden tag at Wexford last time when winning a 3m handicap hurdle off 87 (good to yielding); that was his first try at a 3m trip and he won going away; didn't disgrace himself in his two beginners' chase runs last season; shortlisted.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div I)
Spotlight: Some fair placed form in maiden hurdles last year on good; no impact in a pair of beginners' chases at Kilbeggan recently; cheekpieces need to get more from him on his handicap chase debut.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div I)
Spotlight: Dual hurdles winner won a Tramore beginners' chase in 2024, but is yet to win a handicap; must improve on recent form.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div I)
Spotlight: Sole career win came at Listowel over hurdles in September (2m6f, good to yielding); okay chase debut in a handicap here (2m5f, good to yielding) in May and improved on that when beaten just half a length back at Listowel last time in a beginners' (2m3f, yielding); this trip looks more suitable and she's in the mix.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div I)
Spotlight: Dual winning pointer but his form under rules isn't compelling; pulled up in a handicap chase at Roscommon last time.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div I)
Spotlight: Better hurdler than a chaser, and her last three runs over fences do nothing to dispute that; pulled up at Roscommon on her return.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div I)
Spotlight: Maiden in all spheres after 33 starts, but was second at Downpatrick on penultimate (3m, good), 0.5l behind Fr Gilligansvoyge; sent off favourite up in grade at Wexford last time (2m7f, good), but was a modest third; in the mix on his Downpatrick run, but his record is a concern.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div I)
Spotlight: Running for the 45th time when opening her account over hurdles at Kilbeggan (3m1f, good to yielding, 100-30 fav) last month; couldn't follow up back there next time (3m1f, good, 12-1) off 5lb higher; regular 7lb claimer has been replaced for this return to fences (0-9), but her overall profile is unappealing.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div I)
OwnerName: John P Coleman & M J Caffrey
Spotlight: No wins from 11 attempts over hurdles; one of his better efforts came in first-time cheekpieces at Kilbeggan last time when an 8.25l sixth; that headgear is retained for his chasing bow, but fences need to get more from him.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div I)
Spotlight: Belatedly off the mark in point-to-points in May after 14 attempts; no impact in a handicap chase or hurdle race since then.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div I)
Spotlight: Dual chase winner hasn't visited the winners' enclosure since 2024; tailed off in two chase starts this term; unlikely.

RaceName: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Hooded all starts; won a Chepstow maiden (7f, good) in May on second outing and similar form when second in a Windsor novice (1m, good to firm) last time; better is needed on this handicap/AW debut but early days.

RaceName: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Fine family; no great improvement this term for his maiden win at Kempton (1m, AW) on return, making all as 6-4 favourite, before a respectable show when fifth of eight on handicap debut at Haydock (1m, good to firm; joint favourite) later in April; gelded since and needs overall improvement.

RaceName: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Readily won on Tapeta (1m/7f) in April in his first two handicaps, latterly at this track; well beaten on turf next time, before a respectable fifth of 13 at Kempton (7f) back on AW; may need to resume improvement.

RaceName: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap
Spotlight: Ran poorly at Kempton last August in his only AW race; resumed progress with his turf form on latest outing, winning a 12-runner handicap at Sandown (1m2f, good) five weeks ago when 40-1 in first-time blinkers; up 4lb but that was a warm race and he'll go well if this surface proves no impediment.

RaceName: PKF Wish Race Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Still capable of running up to high-80s RPRs just two years ago, but has offered nothing in two outings this term and remains winless around anywhere as galloping as Nottingham; cheekpieces return.

RaceName: PKF Wish Race Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Dual 5f novice winner in December 2024 but well below that form level these days, faring no better with a tongue-tie added when last seen in March (it's retained here); record when returning from a break is mixed.

RaceName: PKF Wish Race Classified Stakes
OwnerName: John And Tony Jarvis And Partner
Spotlight: Yet to win away from Tapeta, and while June's half-length second in a Doncaster handicap (6f, good to firm) represents his nearest ever miss on turf, it's been diminishing returns from him again since.

RaceName: PKF Wish Race Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 6f turf and Polytrack scorer for Tony Martin, and demonstrated the benefit of her stable debut outing when third at Ffos Las late last month (6f, good); effectiveness over a bare 5f is untested since October 2024.

RaceName: PKF Wish Race Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Sole win in 16 starts was gained in a 6f handicap here (good to firm) 14 months ago, and marginally her best effort since last August came when returning here last month; still early days for her this term and she's not dismissed lightly.

RaceName: PKF Wish Race Classified Stakes
Spotlight: All five turf wins have been gained on galloping sprint courses, and he's netted a neck second (off 64) and a win in handicaps from two C&D starts; yet to pick up a classified in nine attempts, however, and not an unlucky loser in one at Southwell latest despite running short of room.

RaceName: PKF Wish Race Classified Stakes
Spotlight: No pickup in fortunes for the recent switch to classified events (including over 6f here) and application of cheekpieces, well held when third on Tapeta latest.

RaceName: PKF Wish Race Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Breakthrough Carlisle handicap success in May has been flanked by three rather less auspicious efforts, similarly over 6f on good; only one attempt at 5f so far (Tapeta novice stakes at huge odds) but dam and (to Listed level) granddam both scored at this trip.

RaceName: Bet On The Open With BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: Promise on debut (6f, good to firm) but she was easy to back and ran poorly on AW on her second run; back to form with a 7f novice win at Chepstow two weeks ago but that form isn't easy to quantify with the form horses failing to give their running; not bred to need this far but shapes as though it's worth a try.

RaceName: Bet On The Open With BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: Ran as well as she ever has when fourth of 14 on her handicap debut at Southwell (1m, AW) 12 days ago; each-way prospects off the same mark.

RaceName: Bet On The Open With BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: Modest form in three fillies' events from 7f-1m2f; open to improvement now handicapping but she will require a good leap forward.

RaceName: Bet On The Open With BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: Improved her RPR with each of her three runs in AW maiden/novice events (6f-8.6f); Oisin Murphy takes over for her handicap debut and further progress could be on the cards.

RaceName: Bet On The Open With BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: Won twice as a 2yo; improved efforts when in the frame in turf handicaps at Lingfield and over C&D on her last two starts; no obvious reason why she wouldn't give her running again.

RaceName: Bet On The Open With BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: May have needed the run when down the field on her seasonal/handicap debut in May; took a good step forward with her win at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) two weeks ago; up 7lb and stamina for 1m has to be taken on trust but she still has time on her side.

RaceName: Bet On The Open With BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: Placed on four occasions prior to his 7f handicap win at Salisbury in May; that form has worked out well enough to think this mark was in range and his disappointing run here 24 days ago can be excused (lost his position after some early scrimmaging); has to prove his stamina but he will be a danger to all if seeing out the mile.

RaceName: Bet On The Open With BetVictor Handicap
OwnerName: Duaij Al Khalifa & Ahmad Al Qassimi
Spotlight: Promise in maiden/novice events and she ran well for second (of 14; Areti fourth) on her handicap debut at Southwell 12 days ago; 3lb well in and she remains capable of better.

RaceName: Bet On The Open With BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: Absent since a low-key nursery debut at Newmarket (7f, good) 268 days ago; gelded prior to his return; improvement essential for today's step up in trip.

RaceName: Bet On The Open With BetVictor Handicap
Spotlight: Consistent rather than progressive in five runs over sprint trips for William Haggas; returns from 172 days off for Jack Channon (same owners) with a point to prove, not least her stamina.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div II)
Spotlight: Point winner easily won a Tipperary hunter chase in May last year; has been hit or miss since, but one of his better efforts came at Downpatrick last month when taking third (3m, good), making a mistake at two out; way below that level over hurdles at Limerick last time; return to fences should help.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div II)
Spotlight: Winner of a Sligo maiden hurdle in May last year (2m4f, good); beat precious few rivals in four beginners' chases to date; pulled up at Tramore when badly hampered at the first last time; others preferred.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div II)
Spotlight: Won a pair of chases for Charles Byrnes, the latest of them over three years ago; not shown much in hunter chases for this yard and weakened when tried in blinkers in a handicap last time.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div II)
OwnerName: Aiden Larkin & John Larkin & Paudie Dowling & Kenneth Fox
Spotlight: Underwhelming in opening two chase starts before taking a big step forward at Roscommon (2m6f, yielding to soft) to shed his maiden tag convincingly off 90 under today's rider following the reapplication of blinkers; a repeat of that will see him go very close, but overall form suggests that is not a sure thing.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div II)
Spotlight: Winning point-to-pointer got off the mark under rules in a 2m5f Sligo beginners' chase in October (good to yielding) for Gordon Elliott; failed to build on that in three runs afterwards and no sign of a revival over hurdles at Limerick on his stable debut.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div II)
Spotlight: 0-14 in all spheres; has tended to give his best at Downpatrick and was an okay 10.5l fifth of 14 on his handicap chase debut there last time (2m4f, good); needs more.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div II)
Spotlight: 0-20 at the age of nine, but broke a poor run of form back from a break when runner-up over 3m at Bellewstown in April (yielding); pulled up next time and was beaten 24l on his chasing debut at Downpatrick in a beginners' last time; others make more appeal.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div II)
Spotlight: Maiden was placed in a couple of handicap hurdles for Henrietta Knight last year; poor form in opening three chase runs for this yard and return to hurdles last time didn't help.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div II)
Spotlight: Has run poorly since returning from a huge absence last winter; hard to fancy.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div II)
Spotlight: Won a Punchestown handicap hurdle in May (3m, good) off 84 and placed twice since then; made a positive chase debut at Wexford (2m4f, yielding) in April when third off this mark, but pulled up at Tramore next time after making a bad mistake; could go well.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div II)
Spotlight: Point winner and won a Clonmel handicap hurdle in 2024 (2m4f, good); not a lot to recommend him in three runs this season.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div II)
Spotlight: Never a factor in seven hurdle starts; narrow winner of a Ballysteen point-to-point in April; pulled up on handicap chase debut at Limerick, hanging left; an unlikely winner.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div II)
Spotlight: Modest strike rate of 1-21 over fences; took the runner-up spot on consecutive occasions at Limerick and Tramore in April/May, both over 2m6f on good; lesser run back at Tramore when last seen over fences (2m6f, good); poor effort back over hurdles at Limerick earlier this month.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div II)
Spotlight: Has struggled in handicap chases and recent pointing exploits do not bode well.

RaceName: Europe Hotel And Resort Handicap Chase (Div II)
Spotlight: Just 1-21 over hurdles; posted a decent effort on his second chase start at Tramore in May when running on well to take fourth of ten (2m6f, good); others preferred.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Progressive last year, with the second of his two AW wins coming over C&D in December; this is his first run of the year but he has to be of interest.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Of his five AW wins, two have come at this track, one over today's distance; respectable third at Chester (2m, good) on his reappearance in May but needs to be at the top of his game here as he is 5lb above his last winning mark.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Both wins came in 2022 when trained in France; returns to the Flat for the first time this year after a couple of runs over hurdles; down to a career-low mark and has had wind surgery but others are preferred.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: Hot To Trot Racing 1 And Mrs David Egan
Spotlight: Hasn't won since landing a Doncaster novice in July 2024 but has been fairly consistent in handicaps; out of her depth in a Listed contest at Pontefract last time but should run her race here.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Won back to back over 2m for Emma Lavelle last autumn and ran well to make the frame despite racing freely in a couple of Goodwood handicaps in May; one wonders if the drop back in trip will play to his strengths on stable debut but he makes greater appeal than most of these.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Sole success came in April 2025 when trained by Darryl Holland; has performed with credit on several occasions since, resulting in him being still 6lb higher than his winning mark; place claims.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: Hambleton Racing Ltd XXXV & E Burke
Spotlight: Has three wins on turf but has gone off the boil on his last two starts; swaps a visor for first-time cheekpieces on AW debut; not out of this but new headgear needs to have an impact.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: All six wins have come on AW but she is still 3lb above her last winning mark; her recent third at Carlisle (1m6f, good) was encouraging but she needs to step up more to win again.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Has been consistent in defeat in maidens over 5f-6f including once over C&D; best effort came at Bellewstown last time and handles a sound surface well, so is a major player on nursery bow.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Was poor on first two maiden starts but much-improved when third over C&D on soft last time; was nearly 2l in front of Bit Of A Buzz that day but the latter is better suited to drier ground; a major player.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Best effort of three came on debut at Cork over this trip on soft ground; below that level twice since, so questions to be answered about her ability to handle this lively surface.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Soundly beaten in three maidens under varying ground conditions; gelded since last run at Naas, which was his best effort; needs to improve, even tackling nurseries now, but not ruled out.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: All three runs over this trip came within the space of three weeks; best effort came on debut at Naas on good ground; has a shout if resuming progress on nursery debut.

RaceName: Arthur Johnson & Sons Wish Race "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Third at Leicester (good) last time was her most affirmative statement on turf or over 1m2f, as well as being recorded in a fairly well-stocked 0-70; not one for blind faith given her propensity to pull, but this does look an easier opportunity.

RaceName: Arthur Johnson & Sons Wish Race "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)
Spotlight: 0-9; far from his only weak finish when fading back past Revich over 8.5f here in April, and sold out of the Greenall/Guerriero yard for 4,000gns following a similar effort on only run since; last home on sole 1m2f start.

RaceName: Arthur Johnson & Sons Wish Race "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)
Spotlight: 10yo who's the sole winner (seven times) in this, if not since autumn 2023, but last two outings at Bath have advertised the need to give him a longer run over 1m2f now; no ground or track issues, and he's among the likeliest.

RaceName: Arthur Johnson & Sons Wish Race "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Ignore one outpaced effort dropped to 7f and she's basically been consistent, if not the easiest; racing freely appeared more the issue than today's trip at Doncaster latest (0-75) and she remains of interest off a new career-low mark, assuming she helps herself more this time.

RaceName: Arthur Johnson & Sons Wish Race "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Slowly away and generally behind on all three 7f-1m starts (fast turf or standard Tapeta), and now switches to handicaps at a realistic level but off no gift of a mark; winners at 5f-1m4f in immediate pedigree.

RaceName: Arthur Johnson & Sons Wish Race "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won A Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Unable to break out of midfield on any of five Tapeta/Polytrack starts for Jack Channon at up to 8.6f; longer trip is a potential plus given the immediate family is laden with 1m2f winners (many on turf), but cheekpieces need to motivate him more than the visor did; stable debut.

RaceName: Discover Newmarket Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 1,000gns foal by Lope Y Fernandez; fourth foal; half-sister to Polish 7f 2yo winner Damashal; dam 6f winner (RPR 70), out of unraced sister to 7f/1m winner Mustaqbal; best watched on this introduction unless the market suggests otherwise.

RaceName: Discover Newmarket Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: By Dubawi; first foal; dam 7f-10.5f winner (including 2yo/Group 3; RPR 108), half-sister to multiple 9.5f-10.5f Group 1/Grade 1 winner State Of Rest, is granddaughter of 1m2f Listed winner; powerful yard is looking strong in the 2yo department and she's an obvious contender on this debut.

RaceName: Discover Newmarket Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Flattered to get so close when chasing home a stablemate who easily won five-runner Yarmouth novice (7f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; that was still an encouraging first go and she's got the scope to progress.

RaceName: Discover Newmarket Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: The Ten Fools & A Horse Partnership
Spotlight: Sent off 100-1 and the penny appeared to be dropping late on when beaten 16l on her debut over C&D (good) four weeks ago; most likely capable of better but will still need to take a good step forward.

RaceName: Discover Newmarket Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Churchill filly and 44,000euros yearling who went for 170,000euros after a breeze-up in May; first foal; dam 6f/7f winner (including 2yo/AW; RPR 93), half-sister to winners Powerful Breeze (1m 2yo Group 2) and Power To Exceed (6f), out of Listed-placed 1m 2yo winner; a likely newcomer from respected yard.

RaceName: Discover Newmarket Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Too Darn Hot sixth foal; half-sister to winners Raja Raja (1m4f AW; RPR 104) and Chola Empire (7f AW including October 2yo debut; 85); dam 6f/7f AW winner (including 2yo; 83), out of unraced half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Keraka; look for any market clues on this debut.

RaceName: Discover Newmarket Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Mohaather first foal; dam 1m AW winner (RPR 69), half-sister to winners Chocoya (useful 6f including 2yo) and Chiringuita (6f 2yo/7.5f), out of 7f 2yo/1m winner; probably best watched on her first go.

RaceName: Uhy Ross Brooke Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Unexposed 4yo; won a 1m2f maiden on AW last September; weak in the betting prior to her seasonal/handicap debut here (1m2f, good to firm) last month, a race where she plugged on for third; stepping up in trip could unlock more from her but it will need to.

RaceName: Uhy Ross Brooke Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Modest form under both codes for David Pipe; she has proved a different proposition since joining Rod Millman, running away with a small-field Class 5 at Chepstow (1m4f, good to firm) two weeks ago and making light of her penalty at York (1m4f, good to firm) last week; didn't look straightforward under pressure there but she was well on top at the finish and Oisin Murphy replaces an apprentice today; 7lb ahead of her mark and she's a major player.

RaceName: Uhy Ross Brooke Fillies' Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: She has hit the frame in each of her three runs in maiden/novice company, recording best form when second of ten in a 1m6f event at Goodwood four weeks ago; the front pair pulled 12l clear of the third on that occasion and she moves into handicaps with untapped potential.

RaceName: Christy Lucey Transport Mares Finale (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Reappeared last month and much improved from debut run for a previous yard in finishing second at Downpatrick; better again to win Roscommon ladies contest early this month under a positive ride (good); can continue progressing.

RaceName: Christy Lucey Transport Mares Finale (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Footstepsinthesand sister to bumper winner Walk On The Beach; dam unraced sister to useful 2m4f hurdler/chaser Go All The Way; justified favouritism on debut in Limerick mares' bumper in late May (soft), asserting late on despite taking a hold early on; interesting prospect, quicker ground to contend with here.

RaceName: Christy Lucey Transport Mares Finale (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Belardo mare cost 3,000euros as a foal; half-sister to winners Female Approach (11.4f/1m4f) and New Star (Qatari 7.5f-1m2f); dam placed 7f 2yo; unlikely type to make an immediate impact.

RaceName: Christy Lucey Transport Mares Finale (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Mild promise in two point-to-points in the spring for William Codd; debuts for new yard and worth a market check.

RaceName: Christy Lucey Transport Mares Finale (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Signs of ability in point-to-points and on bumper debut last month at Listowel (hooded) over 2m4f (yielding); more needed.

RaceName: Christy Lucey Transport Mares Finale (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Jukebox Jury mare is sister to bumper/2m2f hurdle winner Korinthia; dam bumper winner, sister to bumper/2m4f hurdle winners Baker's Bridge and Backtothekingsnest; top trainer/rider combination and yard have won three of last four renewals, so demands utmost respect.

RaceName: Christy Lucey Transport Mares Finale (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Flat-bred daughter of Free Eagle showed nice bit of improvement on second start when runner-up at Kilbeggan last month (good-to-yielding); didn't run to same level at Wexford, though, hanging left in straight; more needed.

RaceName: Christy Lucey Transport Mares Finale (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Getaway half-sister to winners Rioga Choice (useful 2m hurdle), Its Gonnahappen (2m hurdle) and Special John (3m/3m2f chase); dam 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner; never sighted on Limerick debut in late March on soft; better ground may suit, but can only be watched.

RaceName: Christy Lucey Transport Mares Finale (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Mount Nelson mare cost 4,000euros as a 3yo; half-sister to 2m3f chase winner Opechee; dam placed 2m/2m2f hurdles, half-sister to useful 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Sobers; newcomer likely best watched.

RaceName: Christy Lucey Transport Mares Finale (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Jet Away half-sister to winners Exciting Oscar (3m hurdle) and Chasing Unicorn (bumper); dam unraced half-sister to smart 2m-3m hurdle winner Kilfenora; another newcomer likely best watched, unless market speaks.

RaceName: Christy Lucey Transport Mares Finale (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Looked raw on both runs over the winter, plenty green in Cork bumper and too keen when remote fourth in Limerick mares maiden hurdle; tongue-tied now fitted and encounters much quicker ground.

RaceName: Christy Lucey Transport Mares Finale (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
OwnerName: Mrs Con O'Keeffe & Tadhg O'Sullivan & Anthony O'Keeffe
Spotlight: Half-sister to bumper winner Tim Toe; dam unraced half-sister to useful hurdle/chase winner Abolitionist, out of half-sister to high-class jumper Racing Demon; picked up useful experience when third in an academy hurdle at Naas in December, but following month far too keen and paid the price in Cork bumper; has to settle better and back from sixth months' break; encouraging jockey booking.

RaceName: Christy Lucey Transport Mares Finale (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Sholokhov filly cost 7,500euros as a foal; third foal; dam unraced half-sister to high-class bumper and 2m4f-3m hurdle/chase winner No More Heroes, family high-class jumpers Celestial Gold and Fiveforthree; debutante worth a market check.

RaceName: Joe Mac's Big Retirement Bash Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Course winner (1m5f, good) who went very close at Ripon (1m4f, good) last month; she was last of five here (1m5f, good) last Saturday but was beaten just under 2l having led at a steady pace, and she's not ruled out.

RaceName: Joe Mac's Big Retirement Bash Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: David Kilburn, Mrs Corbett, Chris Wright
Spotlight: She remains 5lb higher than for her last win last August but that's because she's been admirably consistent in defeat this year; runner-up at York (1m4f, good to firm) last Friday and she can be bang there.

RaceName: Joe Mac's Big Retirement Bash Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Ex-Irish 4yo who got off the mark at the ninth attempt at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm) three weeks ago, prevailing by 3l on his second stable start; kept on for close third over 1m3f on his stable debut and there can be optimism that 1m4f will be within range; strong claims up 7lb for last time.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Handicap
Spotlight: Highly tried last year; has shown this mark is within range since handicapping this season, including from wide stalls at Chester; again might have been better drawn on this AW debut (dam did her winning for the yard on turf) in first-time headgear.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Handicap
Spotlight: Currently 3-3 on Tapeta, 0-5 on turf and Polytrack combined; can't be any complaints about a 2lb rise for his Southwell win four weeks ago and he won't mind coming back up from 6f; holds leading claims.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Handicap
Spotlight: 2-2 on the AW last backend (Polytrack, Tapeta) and holds claims switched back from turf judged on either of her recent Carlisle efforts (among the favourites for a Listed race last time); yard among the winners lately too, which is a bonus.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Handicap
Spotlight: Absent for almost five months, having been gelded following two lesser efforts (raced exclusively on the AW to date); yard enjoyed a good month thus far and it will be interesting to see how he goes in the market.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Handicap
Spotlight: Not beaten far in a couple of sales races last autumn; not as good in two runs back, getting turned over at odds-on round Redcar before not seeing out 8.6f here early last month, and will need plenty more on this handicap debut.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Handicap
Spotlight: Three ordinary efforts on turf punctuate four solid efforts on the AW, including when coming away with a subsequent easy winner over C&D off 2lb lower in the spring; may well be dropped in again from this draw and will be happier back up from 6f; one to consider.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Handicap
Spotlight: Plenty of racing last year, when finishing well held in two runs on Tapeta; offered a bit more on latest start but still has stamina to prove, having just a second go beyond 6f, and this race is more competitive.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Handicap
OwnerName: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
Spotlight: 7f Tapeta winner off 4lb lower last backend before two lesser efforts in a hood (pulled hard on one occasion; headgear now comes off); some way down the pecking order on his return from a break.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On Instagram Handicap
Spotlight: Won a less competitive C&D handicap off 2lb lower in May; again inclined to over-race when upped to 1m on turf at Windsor last time, which was another solid effort all things considered but he's vulnerable to improvers in this better race.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
Spotlight: Got off the mark over this trip at Dundalk in March and a solid fourth back there next time; put in a cracking effort from the front on turf debut at Bellewstown behind an in-form rival; up 3lb but can improve from that; big player.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden raced mostly on AW for Natalia Lupini and some solid runs up to 1m; the best of two runs for this yard when fourth over C&D on soft a month ago; a player if as effective on this faster ground.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden after 14 runs but some fine efforts in defeat, all over this trip; solid effort again at Bellewstown last time in first-time cheekpieces (retained), just behind Bye Bye Now; a player but needs more.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
Spotlight: C&D winner in 2023 won for the second time in a 6f Fairyhouse claimer in May; raised 19lb for that win but has been solid in defeat since including over C&D; got no run at Fairyhouse last time; a player.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
Spotlight: The best of three maiden runs last year came over C&D; lacks experience for a race of this nature, but yard does well with sprinters and could go well on return.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden hasn't built on early promise, but some good runs all the same between turf and AW; third in a big field at the Curragh two starts ago, but a lesser effort on soft over 7f here since; can do better.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
Spotlight: Not beaten far a couple of times in maidens, but didn't progress in AW handicaps over the winter; has to return to best on stable debut after a break.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden after 32 starts has been mostly running well this season, but getting bumped up in the handicap for being placed isn't helping matters; likely to run well again.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
Spotlight: Some promise in maidens; best handicap run came last time at Bellewstown over this trip when ran on from the rear; hasn't much to find with Bye Bye Now on that run; added visor could help.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
Spotlight: Showed improved form lately, winning over 6f at Fairyhouse but a lesser effort at Limerick over 6.5f since; should have the speed for this trip, but a few other front-runners in this field too.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
Spotlight: Regressive maiden but ran a cracker over this trip at Naas before a lesser run over 6.5f at Limerick since; cheekpieces retained; back to 5f will suit but needs more.

RaceName: Racing Welfare Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: The Boys Are Back In Town
Spotlight: He raced seven times during last season's 2yo campaign and showed only very modest form; looks up against it on his reappearance.

RaceName: Racing Welfare Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Unseated rider and slowly away when 11l fifth of seven at 20-1 on debut at Nottingham (8.3f, good) in May; this 45,000gns yearling is open to improvement but may be of greater interest when tackling further.

RaceName: Racing Welfare Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Made debut last month and not devoid of ability on his two starts (1m2f, soft/good); however, soundly beaten each time and lots of improvement is needed on stable debut; sold out of Andrew Balding's yard for 10,000gns.
