RaceName: Racing Welfare Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Sent off at 8-1 on debut at Chepstow (7f, good to firm) a fortnight ago and kept on for second, albeit no match for the winner; a chunk of improvement is needed if he's to bother Cerro Blanco but it was a promising start to his career and he should have more to offer.

RaceName: Racing Welfare Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 1,800gns foal; first foal; dam 7f 2yo winner (RPR 99) for connections, half-sister to three winners, out of 8.6f 2yo winner from talented family; a market check is advised but this Advertise newcomer may be best watched.

RaceName: Racing Welfare Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: L J Vaessen And Stephen Barber
Spotlight: Left her debut run behind when eighth of 11 at 250-1 at Yarmouth (7f, good to soft) last month but was still soundly beaten and she needs another big step forward today.

RaceName: Crossland Employment Solicitors Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Best efforts on AW, including second at Newcastle (1m2f) late last month; never dangerous when 16-1 at Sandown (1m2f, good to firm) nine days later, but today's race is much less competitive than his other two turf handicaps this term.

RaceName: Crossland Employment Solicitors Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Minor honours over 1m3f/1m4f in his two Flat handicaps this term (his last runs for Alan King) prior to his creditable second 19 days ago for new yard over hurdles; should be a serious player.

RaceName: Crossland Employment Solicitors Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Unraced on good to firm except on debut (fourth); won twice from five starts as 2yo and creditable fourth of ten here on reappearance; faded right out of it at Newmarket (also 1m on good) four weeks later, which sees him visored today with a step up in trip; may need overall improvement.

RaceName: Crossland Employment Solicitors Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Didn't look straightforward when the race got serious on last two outings but he was nevertheless better at keeping a straight line when cheekpieces were enlisted for six-runner maiden at Sandown (1m2f, good) last time and stuck his neck out well to win by 1l; some improvement is probably needed going handicapping but he has to be considered seriously in this small field.

RaceName: Crossland Employment Solicitors Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: A string of minor honours but no win from his eight races; disappointing on good to firm at Ascot in May, in between showing his best form at Kempton (1m3f, AW) and Sandown (1m2f, good); blinkers were fitted for that latest start, when he came from the back for fifth of 12 despite carrying his head high, and this race is markedly less competitive.

RaceName: Babs Woolford Memorial Apprentice Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Resumed winning ways in classified event at Wolverhampton (9.5f) in March; winless on turf though and only eighth of 13 in 1m Ripon handicap last time; others appeal more.

RaceName: Babs Woolford Memorial Apprentice Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Sole success came in June 2024 but he arrives in decent nick, fifth of 11 in C&D handicap ten days ago; not out of things.

RaceName: Babs Woolford Memorial Apprentice Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Is 0-26 but comes here in good form, third of 11 in C&D handicap ten days ago; needs considering dropping back into classified company.

RaceName: Babs Woolford Memorial Apprentice Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Twice second (over 1m2f) in classified company at Chelmsford over the winter but his more recent form is not so encouraging, sixth of 13 in 1m2f classified event at Newcastle last time; on a long losing run too.

RaceName: Babs Woolford Memorial Apprentice Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Waldgeist filly who has advanced her form this season, fourth in 1m2f handicap here before gaining a first success at Beverley (1m4f) last month; she can go well again back in classified company.

RaceName: Babs Woolford Memorial Apprentice Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Scored at Southwell (1m4f) in March but only ninth of 11 in handicap over C&D ten days ago; not proving the easiest to catch right of late.

RaceName: Babs Woolford Memorial Apprentice Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Lightly raced Sea The Moon gelding who took a big step forward on his handicap debut with a clear second of eight in 1m4f handicap at Ripon 11 days ago; holds very good claims at these weights.

RaceName: Babs Woolford Memorial Apprentice Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Yet to get her head in front and she was tried in cheekpieces when sixth of eight in 1m4f handicap at Ripon 11 days ago; needs to get back on track.

RaceName: Jordan Electrics EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands A, B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 75,000gns breeze-up buy who was sent off the 4-9 favourite when a head second of four on debut at Ayr (6f, good) 12 days ago; Trumpety Trump was half a length behind and is 5lb better off today, but this fellow is open to improvement for his top yard and could play a leading role.

RaceName: Jordan Electrics EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands A, B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Backed into 11-4 when keeping on for clear second on debut at Beverley (5f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; shaped as though the step up to 6f could be a positive move and he's firmly in calculations.

RaceName: Jordan Electrics EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands A, B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: £95,000 breeze-up buy who finished among some pricey recruits when runner-up at 9-1 on recent debut at Nottingham (6f, good to firm); that was a promising start and there is every chance that she'll progress; key player.

RaceName: Jordan Electrics EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands A, B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: £28,000 breeze-up buy who was a close third of four at 33-1 on recent debut at Ayr (6f, good); he's 5lb better off with the runner-up Instant Force and his trainer isn't known for first-time-out 2yo winners, so it was a very encouraging start to his career and he could have lots more to offer.

RaceName: Jordan Electrics EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands A, B, C And D) (EBF Restricted Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Failed to beat a rival last month on her first two starts (6f/5f, soft/good to firm), when 20-1 then 50-1; she would be a shock winner today.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Promise on debut at the Curragh over 6f behind a smart winner; a lesser run at Listowel since; the step up in trip should suit on breeding and looks to be the yard's first-string, but has to resume progress.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Beaten a long way in two maidens and will be better off in nurseries after this; visor added.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden
Spotlight: A Eur500,000 yearling; started a short-priced favourite on debut at Navan over 6f but never landed a blow; marginal improvement upped to 7f at Leopardstown since; has to do more today.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Promise on debut at Leopardstown over 7f (soft) and built on that over a mile at Bellewstown on good ground; cheekpieces are added today and can be a major player,  though the drop in trip is a concern.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Both starts came at Roscommon over a similar trip to this; not beaten far on debut, but produced a lesser effort last time; seems to be the yard's second-string, but can be involved if resuming progress.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Similar efforts in 6f maidens at Cork and Navan the last twice; has to do more up in trip and mixed messages about stamina in pedigree.

RaceName: Racing TV Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Last won when sharing the spoils at Southwell (1m4f, AW) over 15 months ago but he was in good form this spring and can be excused his latest effort on account of soft ground; ran well on his only previous visit here too; considered.

RaceName: Racing TV Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Has made good strides across three AW starts, latterly winning four-runner novice at Newcastle (1m2f) in January; goes up in trip again for this turf/handicap debut and market support would increase confidence on this first start since.

RaceName: Racing TV Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Still looked a touch raw when bagging 1m4f Catterick maiden (good) in April but he didn't build on that when fourth of five on his handicap debut at Doncaster four weeks ago; still early days and the added blinkers may help.

RaceName: Racing TV Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: She is another that has been getting her act together, most recently winning a five-runner Thirsk novice (1m4f, good) one month ago; opening mark looks reasonable; leading candidate.

RaceName: G3 Vehicle Auctions EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: G H Thompson And Michael Dods
Spotlight: 11-1, shaped well when a debut third of ten in maiden at Leicester (5f, good to firm) in May, keeping on late; this longer trip promises to suit this son of Minzaal so he's a big player.

RaceName: G3 Vehicle Auctions EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Naval Crown colt; promising runner-up in 5f Leicester maiden in May but came in last of six in 5f maiden here 30 days ago; since gelded and no surprise to see him bounce back.

RaceName: G3 Vehicle Auctions EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Proving a fair and reliable maiden until beating just two in 6f Leicester seller 13 days ago; needs to take a step forward if he's to get off the mark at the ninth attempt.

RaceName: G3 Vehicle Auctions EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Bated Breath colt who has hinted at promise on both his runs, not enjoying the smoothest of passages with seventh of 11 in 7f Carlisle maiden 23 days ago; looks to be one for the longer term.

RaceName: G3 Vehicle Auctions EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar
Spotlight: 15-2, made an encouraging start with second of six in maiden at Ayr (6f, soft) 27 days ago; this son of Persian Force is open to progress and must enter calculations.

RaceName: G3 Vehicle Auctions EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Sent off 28-1 but showed some promise when a debut fourth of six in novice at Catterick (5f, good to firm) nine days ago; this Harry Angel filly can take a step forward.

RaceName: G3 Vehicle Auctions EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 17,000gns yearling, 3,500gns 2yo breeze-ups; half-sister to winners Havana Angel (French Group-placed 5.5f 2yo; RPR 88) and Foothold (1m; 69); dam, 1m4f winner (69), is a half-sister to 6f 2yo Listed winner Sweet Cecily; hooded for her debut and the market can guide for this Naval Crown filly.

RaceName: Hamish MacGregor Memorial Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Showed improved form when getting off the mark at the fourth attempt at Pontefract (6f, good) last month; unsuited by the step up to 7.4f at Beverley since and she could play a leading role back down in trip with a tongue-tie added on nursery debut.

RaceName: Hamish MacGregor Memorial Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Took a step forward with 4l third of six at Ayr (6f, soft) on her second start and similar form when sixth of 11, beaten about 3l, at Doncaster (6f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; not obviously well treated off his opening mark but his pedigree provides optimism that he'll improve.

RaceName: Hamish MacGregor Memorial Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Improved upon her first two runs (5f, good to soft/good) when winning a five-runner maiden at Ffos Las (5f, soft) last month in first-time cheekpieces (retained) and she did it in good style; second of three in a 5f nursery here yesterday; the step up to 6f seems a likely plus; respected.

RaceName: Hamish MacGregor Memorial Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Kept on well to win at Beverley (5f, good to soft) in May on second start and returned from a break with good second at 50-1 on stable debut at York (6f, good to firm) last Saturday; leading claims off the same mark.

RaceName: Hamish MacGregor Memorial Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Promising second when 33-1 on debut at Southwell (5f, AW) in April and a market check is advised on this first nursery start, but two lesser runs have followed (5f, good to firm/good) and she needs to get back on track.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Maiden
Spotlight: Dandy Man 4yo is brother to Italian 6f-7.5f winner; cost 7,000 euros as a yearling; dam 6f winner, out of US 7f/1m winning half-sister to US 1m Grade 2 winner Habaya; respected yard, but best watched unless money arrives.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Maiden
Spotlight: Well beaten both starts; will be in handicaps after this.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Maiden
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: Second at Galway festival and Gowran third to this year's Derby winner Christmas Day were the pick of his 2yo soft-ground maiden efforts; disappointed on seasonal return in Curragh handicap; back from a break with much better effort over 1m on good ground at Leopardstown last week; cheekpieces stay on; Aga Khan damline suggests he'll stay, but that remains to be seen.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Maiden
Spotlight: River Boyne gelding from good family; 66s, sixth to Irish 2,000 Guineas also-ran Bamako Beach over 7f at Fairyhouse, but he was beaten miles.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Maiden
Spotlight: Ghaiyyath colt from a talented Juddmonte family; 18s for quick-ground 1m2f Curragh maiden on Derby weekend, showed ability when beaten 5l by winner who now sits on a mark of 88; spoilt his chance significantly by hanging left up the straight; form of that run yet to be tested.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Maiden
Spotlight: Front-running third over 1m2f at Leopardstown; weakened over 1m4f on testing ground at Cork; third over C&D last month and the second easily went one better at Killarney on Monday; has only run on yielding or worse; headgear.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Maiden
OwnerName: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith
Spotlight: Ran well in red-hot 1m maiden at Naas on her return; again stable second-string (20-1) when running much the same race over same C&D last month; dam 1m2f Listed winner, sister to Irish Derby winner Latrobe, so Dubawi filly is bred to get this trip, although the visual evidence from her two runs this year is inconclusive in that regard.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Maiden
Spotlight: Nice Jim Bolger pedigree but showed nothing in 1m4f maiden on debut; tongue-tie discarded.

RaceName: National Stud Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: His only race on firmer than good was on debut, when runner-up; sole win came in a 1m Ffos Las maiden as 3yo; that's from nine races and despite being favourite in his last five handicaps but all three starts this season (1m2f, 1m and 1m1f; never nearer at evens last time) confirmed that he is well capable of being seriously involved.

RaceName: National Stud Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Sole win from 16 races came in France (9.5f, AW) as a 2yo; sinking in the weights and well handicapped judged on plenty of 2025 French form, with a step back in the right direction when fifth of 14 at Sandown (1m2f) on penultimate start; not at all discredited in a hot race here 13 days ago when the move up to 1m4f may have stretched him; wore first-time cheekpieces that day and blinkers are now given a go; not dismissed.

RaceName: National Stud Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: He's had a long career but never been better than this season, winning at Wolverhampton (9.4f) and Lingfield (1m4f) with a close third in between; they were all on AW in March/April under Olivia Tubb and he's not raced since, with turf runs in the last two years hinting that he's not quite so good in this sphere; also, the last time he raced on firmer than good was 2022.

RaceName: National Stud Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Made the running when 1m2f handicap winner (good to firm) this year at Doncaster and Beverley; 25-1 and again set the pace when fourth of five in Listed race at Beverley (1m4f) 13 days ago, which was another good run but whether it's worth another 3lb rise in today's mark can be questioned.

RaceName: National Stud Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: AW and dual turf winner over about 1m2f last April-June; some minor honours in his November-March campaign in the UAE, mostly on dirt, without reproducing best efforts; tongue tied first time for today's return from four months off and he needs to be back at his peak; has a bit to prove on firmer than good (after one attempt) but his yard is among the winners.

RaceName: National Stud Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Dual AW winner last November who also took a handicap at Newmarket (1m, good) in April; unsurprisingly lost her unbeaten record in the 1,000 Guineas (80-1) and her handicap at Royal Ascot (again 1m on good to firm; 25-1) was pretty tough too but she was well beaten in the latter; this should be vastly more manageable and there's more than enough stamina in her family to boost interest.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
Spotlight: Arrives in fine form and bidding for a hat-trick following back-to-back 6f wins here, emphatically making all on latter occasion; has won over the minimum trip too so rates a big player under a 4lb penalty.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
Spotlight: A fairly useful ex-Irish 5f winner; yet to hit top form for his current yard but better signs when ninth of 14 in 5f Newcastle handicap three weeks ago; no forlorn hope off a 2lb lower mark if building on it.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
Spotlight: Much improved when an easy winner at Wolverhampton (5f) in February but well below par in Chantilly Listed race (5.5f) and handicap at Royal Ascot (5f) since; no surprise to see him bounce back here.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
Spotlight: Is enjoying an excellent season and gained a third success when making all in 5f Beverley handicap two weeks ago; up 3lb but not taken lightly in his hat-trick bid.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
Spotlight: Took this prize in 2024; back in fine form of late since fitted with blinkers, scoring at Hamilton (5f) in May and in the frame in big-field 5f handicaps at Thirsk and Newcastle since; ought to be thereabouts.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
Spotlight: Gained a first win of 2026 in 5f handicap at Ripon 11 days ago; usual visor goes back on now and this four-time C&D scorer is not out of things under a 4lb penalty.

RaceName: Sky Bet Go-Racing-In-Yorkshire Summer Festival Handicap
Spotlight: Right back to his best of late, landing back-to-back C&D handicaps; this is tougher under a 5lb penalty but he can't be dismissed in his hat-trick attempt.

RaceName: Jordan Electrics Scottish Stewards' Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Unraced on turf this year and his best form has come on AW; however, he has a lower mark on grass and was third in a Listed race at Newmarket (6f, good to firm) last August; capable of playing a leading role.

RaceName: Jordan Electrics Scottish Stewards' Cup Handicap
OwnerName: Doug Graham, Ian Davison, Alan Drysdale
Spotlight: 9yo whose last win was in September 2023 and it's possible that he'll need this first outing since February; however, he won this in 2021 and showed spark when twice finishing second in Bahrain this January (6f, good/good to firm); not ruled out.

RaceName: Jordan Electrics Scottish Stewards' Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Got up in the final strides over 5f here (good to firm) three weeks ago on his fourth start for this yard; he could have more to offer for this stable, especially back up in trip with his Irish wins having come at 7.5f/7f; respected up 4lb for last time.

RaceName: Jordan Electrics Scottish Stewards' Cup Handicap
Spotlight: 8yo who can be in and out but he won at Leicester (6f, good to firm) in April and has run well in defeat on his last two starts, at Epsom (6f, soft) and Newcastle (6f, AW); he was a close second of 14 last time and this C&D winner can be in the mix if he's once more on a going day.

RaceName: Jordan Electrics Scottish Stewards' Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Having dropped down the weights he was a close second at Ascot (6f, good) in May on his stable/seasonal debut, and perhaps the soft ground didn't suit at Epsom last time; not written off but this 8yo has been regressive and remains without a win since June 2023.

RaceName: Jordan Electrics Scottish Stewards' Cup Handicap
OwnerName: B Jordan, Brian Jordan, S Jordan & N McConnell
Spotlight: 10yo who is winless since August 2024 and needs to improve upon his last few performances; however, he's a nine-time course winner, including this race in 2024, and his reduced mark appeals on form as recent as this May; not discounted.

RaceName: Jordan Electrics Scottish Stewards' Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Running well this year, including a close third here (5f, good) in May, and he posted a deserved win in a big field at Ayr (6f, good) last Monday; this C&D winner is a solid candidate under a 4lb penalty.

RaceName: Jordan Electrics Scottish Stewards' Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Reappeared with a win at Catterick (6f, good) in April and then made all at Epsom (6f, good) this month; his stable is a dab hand when it comes to older horses progressing through the ranks and, with the Epsom form having been franked, a 3lb rise could prove lenient for this thriving 5yo.

RaceName: GAIN The Advantage Series Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Consistent on AW at three, winning over 1m4f; had some good hurdling form, without winning, in 2024; just one run over flights last year; ran well in a charity race at the Curragh on Derby weekend; has to enter calculations in such a weak contest.

RaceName: GAIN The Advantage Series Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Placed at two and went close to making all in 1m4f Leopardstown handicap last year, only beaten half-a-length; left Natalia Lupini after; tailed off on stable debut last month.

RaceName: GAIN The Advantage Series Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up in soft-ground 1m4f handicap at Doncaster at three for Charlie Johnston; lightly raced for this yard; okay run over hurdles last year; out the back in 1m2f Roscommon handicap when last seen.

RaceName: GAIN The Advantage Series Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: AW winner over 10.5f in March and her higher-grade third at Naas was a good effort; 1m4f has seemed a bridge too far, though, and long way behind Masego over that trip at Bellewstown; excuse for latest run (1m2f) at Navan.

RaceName: GAIN The Advantage Series Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Five wins, two of them on turf, including 25-1 success over extended 1m6f at Bellewstown earlier this month; needed every yard that day, and his previous win was over 2m; got an 8lb rise, which is likely to prevent a follow-up; placed once in seven previous runs here.

RaceName: GAIN The Advantage Series Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Trainer's four-timer last weekend makes this 1m1f winner of interest; narrowly beaten over 1m3f here in September, too, and some decent runs hurdling during the winter; nice run over 1m at Leopardstown but unplaced at Navan since; tongue-tie left off; doubt she wants it too fast.

RaceName: GAIN The Advantage Series Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Bit of promise over hurdles last year for Mags Mullins; made all to beat 18 rivals over this trip on good ground at Leopardstown; little wrong with stable debut third over 1m4f at Bellewstown (second touched off since); 2lb wrong matters little and front-runner is a player.

RaceName: Madness Handicap
Spotlight: He's on a hat-trick in this race and followed last year's win by also scoring at Windsor and Yarmouth (all 5f on good to firm in cheekpieces) in August; no wins since but he didn't get much cover when midfield over C&D one week ago and his race record means he can't be discounted; also ran well sole previous go in a visor.

RaceName: Madness Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins came on AW but he's fine on turf and it was a career best on turf when second of nine here (6f, good to firm) four weeks ago; might have been flattered by bagging the near rail that day but he won his only previous start at 5f and can't be dismissed.

RaceName: Madness Handicap
Spotlight: Six wins, all at 5f, include five on turf, most recently when making a winning reappearance at Nottingham (good) in April; slight concern that his sole run here was his worst but he's won off this mark and looks a player.

RaceName: Madness Handicap
Spotlight: Three wins include a successful stable debut at Brighton (5.5f, good to firm) and he wasn't beaten far under a penalty when fourth of five at Lingfield (5f, AW) three weeks ago; career-high mark but he's respected.

RaceName: Madness Handicap
Spotlight: Ran out of puff having lost a shoe at Windsor on his comeback last month and he again faded having attempted to make all over C&D (good to firm) 19 days later; becoming attractively handicapped, however, and could be building up to something bigger.

RaceName: Madness Handicap
Spotlight: Started well for this yard but the wheels have come off lately, finishing last over C&D one month ago then finishing tailed off on AW last Monday; not easy to suggest after that.

RaceName: Madness Handicap
Spotlight: Good run of form in the spring included successive wins on AW in April and solid second on turf (also 6f wearing eye-shields) in May; not so good subsequently and he couldn't justify a strong market move back on AW a fortnight ago; bit to prove on this drop back to 5f but last time was a step back in the right direction.

RaceName: Madness Handicap
Spotlight: 0-13 and he hasn't suggested he's about to cash in on a reduced mark, including when sixth of ten at Carlisle (7f) last Thursday; he's never gone closer than when a neck second at Musselburgh last August on his sole start at 5f, however, which could be a straw to clutch to.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Ended 2025 with 1m Southwell win and largely in good nick this season, seventh of 14 in 1m Carlisle handicap last month; not ruled out from a slipping mark.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Gained a deserved first win since 2024 in 1m apprentices' handicap at Ascot a week ago, when well on top at the finish; escapes a penalty and is able to race off 2lb lower than last time, so he's very hard to side against.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Often slowly away but not so when gaining a first success of 2026 in seven-runner handicap over C&D 18 days ago; up 3lb but still not out of things.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Kickstarted 2026 with C&D win in April and posted another solid effort with third of nine over C&D ten days ago; considered off the same mark.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: A dual winner (around 1m) in 2025 but he's yet to fire this season, only ninth of 11 in 1m Redcar handicap four weeks ago; others appeal more.

RaceName: Sky Bet For The Fans Handicap
Spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who signed off for Oliver Cole with a very good second of ten in 1m2f handicap at Chelmsford in November; since changed hands for 10,000gns and needs to hit the ground running for new yard on her belated return.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Glasgow Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Won Redcar novice (1m2f, good to firm) in April on reappearance before respectable third of five in Group 3 Chester Vase (1m4f, good), behind subsequent Irish Derby winner Benvenuto Cellini; early interference when down the field at Royal Ascot and could have a part to play back down in grade.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Glasgow Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Built upon the promise of his sole 2yo run to win Leicester maiden (1m2f, good to firm) on reappearance, and he followed up in a small-field novice at Newmarket (1m2f, good) last month; today is a much tougher test of his credentials but this blue-blooded colt may well be up to the task.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Glasgow Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Five unsuccessful starts for Joseph O'Brien, finishing second in a couple of 2yo maidens; changed hands for 2,500gns this month and she would be a surprise winner on her stable debut, for which a hood is applied.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Glasgow Stakes (Listed Race)
OwnerName: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum
Spotlight: Won three handicaps in a row in May/June (1m2f/10.3f, good/soft); down the field in Royal Ascot handicap next time and same story there last Friday, so she needs to turn things around, but she's not discounted in view of her previous promise.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Glasgow Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Made it two wins from her four starts when easily winning a Class 3 handicap here (1m1f, good) in May; stamina to prove today and she was only fifth in a Listed race at Goodwood (1m2f, soft) last time, but the ground may not have suited and she remains one to be interested in, especially back here.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Glasgow Stakes (Listed Race)
Spotlight: Two-time AW novice winner (1m/1m4f) who was runner-up in the Lingfield Oaks Trial (11.6f, good to firm) before respectable fifth of 15 in the Italian Oaks (10.5f, good); the Lingfield form hasn't worked out but she's nevertheless a leading contender today.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Fillies & Mares Handicap
Spotlight: Won a 1m4f maiden at Bellewstown last August on second start; first two runs this season were solid, but was poor at Ballinrobe last time; only dropped 1lb and needs more, but cheekpieces might help.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Fillies & Mares Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins apiece on turf and AW up to 1m4f; best turf effort this term when second at Gowran last month; not as good over 1m2f twice since; stepping back up to this trip will suit and cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Fillies & Mares Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs Tracey O'Hare & D W Sloan & William Jones
Spotlight: Sole win came here in last year's Ulster Cesarewitch over 2m2f; a fine effort over this trip back here next time; however, has been well below her best in three runs this term; has to rebound; tongue-tie added.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Fillies & Mares Handicap
Spotlight: Has shown improved form lately when winning twice at this track over extended 1m2f, a maiden on good ground and a 3yo handicap on soft; raised 13lb for latter success, but still has to be taken seriously; should stay this trip.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Fillies & Mares Handicap
Spotlight: The last of her three wins came at Ballinrobe off 14lb higher nearly two years ago; has been regressive in the last year and needs to do more, even off a falling mark

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: 2-2 in AW novices last year and she retained her unbeaten record on handicap debut at Doncaster (also 6f, good to firm) in May; drop to 5f and soft ground didn't see her replicate her best in Ayr Listed event four weeks ago but better can be expected in this scenario.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: She rattled off a hat-trick at 6f on good ground around this time last year and was back in the groove when scoring at Ayr (6f, soft) four weeks ago; has been put back to her career-high mark (0-2) but there's a solid chance she can defy it this time.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Constant progress last year saw her win four nurseries (5f, sound turf and AW) and she completed her season with very creditable fourth of 19 in a Listed event at the other Newmarket track (6f, good) in October; look for market interest ahead of this reappearance.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Has a very good C&D record with three wins (all good to firm) last term; had dipped below her last winning mark when attempting to make it four three weeks ago, so her sixth of seven behind Powdering was underwhelming; dropped a further 2lb and she's not readily dismissed.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Dual 6f AW novice winner who was competing in just her third handicap when posting a career best for second at Southwell (6f, AW) off this mark four weeks ago; needs to carry that progress back to turf but she's unexposed and likely capable of better again.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Multiple winner at up to 1m who scored for the ninth time when landing a not dissimilar event over C&D three weeks ago; has never won off a mark this high but seems to be getting quicker as she ages and has gone back-to-back before.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Beat five others to win this race off 3lb higher last year and she chased home Powdering over C&D three weeks ago; bombed out in a better race over 7f here on Saturday but that might be best ignored; visor enlisted.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Won Goodwood novice (6f, good) in October for William Haggas and her handicap debut the following month can be written off (lame); joined her new yard for 6,000gns at the start of this month and best watched unless the market speaks positively.

RaceName: Peter Chamberlain Five Year Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: A four-time 6f AW scorer in 2025; built on earlier promise to gain a first turf victory in 7f Chester handicap a week ago, making all and drawing right away last 1f for 8l success; major shout under a 5lb penalty.

RaceName: Peter Chamberlain Five Year Memorial Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs S Hibbert, Foulrice Park Racing Ltd
Spotlight: Yet to score this term but running well, fourth of ten in 5.5f Doncaster handicap eight days ago; he ought to be thereabouts once more.

RaceName: Peter Chamberlain Five Year Memorial Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs C M Clarke, Foulrice Park Racing Ltd
Spotlight: Often a slow starter but he got away on level terms when landing 5.5f Doncaster handicap eight days ago; not out of things under a 5lb penalty.

RaceName: Peter Chamberlain Five Year Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Scored at Redcar (6f) last May and signed off for 2025 with a good third of 13 over C&D in October; needs to hit the ground running after nine months off though.

RaceName: Peter Chamberlain Five Year Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: On a long losing sequence but he got back on track with second of 11 in 6f Carlisle handicap eight days ago; needs to reproduce it here.

RaceName: Peter Chamberlain Five Year Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Back to winning ways at Redcar (6f) in May and has continued in good nick, third of 12 there (6f) last time; this C&D scorer needs considering.

RaceName: Hamilton Park Celebrates 100 Years Of Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Made all twice in a row this spring (including over C&D on good to firm) and bounced back from his third of six at Yarmouth for another front-running win at Lingfield (5f, AW) last Monday; had plenty in hand last time and he's 5lb well in under a 5lb penalty; could prove tough to catch.

RaceName: Hamilton Park Celebrates 100 Years Of Racing Handicap
Spotlight: 6yo who has enjoyed a renaissance in 2026, winning twice (5f, AW/good) and posting a close second at Ripon (5f, good) latest; there remains mileage in his mark judged on old form and this course winner is respected.

RaceName: Hamilton Park Celebrates 100 Years Of Racing Handicap
Spotlight: She's been running well in the main this season and has finished second at Ayr (5f, good/good to firm) on her two starts this month; she's won over C&D and there's every chance that she'll be bang there.

RaceName: Hamilton Park Celebrates 100 Years Of Racing Handicap
Spotlight: 3yo who won at Newcastle (5f, AW) in January on his second stable start but slim pickings across his three runs since; upset in stalls and withdrawn at Musselburgh in June (33-1 at the time); wears a first-time hood.

RaceName: Hamilton Park Celebrates 100 Years Of Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Returned to form when making all in first-time cheekpieces (retained since) in a selling handicap over C&D (good to firm) last month; lost a shoe when down the field at Ayr (5f, good to firm) on Monday but this is a hotter race than the one he won here two starts ago and others are more compelling.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
Spotlight: Well-bred son of Frankel; couple of respectable maiden runs; only 15-2 for handicap debut over 1m4f at Limerick, a race won easily by stablemate and favourite; dropped out from a bad draw and couldn't go on after making a bit of headway early in the straight; blinkers.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
OwnerName: Anthony Head & Stephen E McCarthy & Mrs Paul Shanahan
Spotlight: No worries about the trip for this stoutly-bred filly; best of three handicap runs was front-running fourth over 1m4f at Cork in April; winner has gone in again twice and third won maiden next time; was given a break after Roscommon fifth in May.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
Spotlight: Quiet in maidens and unable to land a blow when 50s for 1m4f handicap at Limerick; still early days for Snellen's half-brother; dam was 1m4f winner and is half-sister to dam of King George winner Novellist, so no surprise they're giving him another go over a trip.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
Spotlight: Showed ability in a Galway maiden last year when with Mick Mulvany; well backed into joint-favouritism for return at Fairyhouse, but didn't feature; off the bridle 3f out when okay fifth here over 10.5f (soft); had a wide trip from a bad draw when fourth over 1m3f at Naas and reckon he's on a good mark now; not bred to stay, but might.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
Spotlight: Had fair AW form in headgear for Charlie Johnston during the winter, winning over 1m2f and runner-up over 1m4f; better on third Irish start when fourth against older rivals at Fairyhouse (1m2f); starting to stay on from rear when carried wide on the home bend at Ballinrobe; stepping back up in trip might help and can lead.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
Spotlight: Flopped when 3-1 for seasonal/handicap debut at Sligo in May; it was probably soft ground, rather than this trip, which proved his undoing because (having been gelded and given a break) he ran much better over 1m3f at Killarney on Monday; looked slow but kept plugging away, and gets another change of headgear now.

RaceName: Ampion Electrical Testing And Solar Experts Handicap
Spotlight: Twice made the frame in soft-ground 7f Gowran handicaps in the spring; finished well when fourth against older horses over 1m1f on fast ground at Leopardstown, but bombed over 1m3f at Naas last time; yet to string two good runs together and trip is a big question mark.

RaceName: Burwell Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Has been revitalised since joining this yard with form figures of 23121; latest win came in this headgear combination at Yarmouth (1m, good to firm) last Wednesday; drop back to 7f is no problem and she's a leading candidate under the penalty.

RaceName: Burwell Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Last win came at Yarmouth (7f, good to firm) in May 2025; has slipped to a dangerous mark and any market support should be noted.

RaceName: Burwell Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Can miss the break but she won this race last year off 10lb lower and swiftly got over a Chester aberration when second of 11 here (1m, good to firm) under Arron D'Arcy four weeks ago; a player.

RaceName: Burwell Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Solid strike-rate on AW but not so good on turf and latest sixth of 12 at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) will need improving on; nothing wrong with this mark, though.

RaceName: Burwell Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win on turf came over two years ago and his last two efforts have been moderate; this mark is fine if first-time cheekpieces spark him back to life, however.

RaceName: Burwell Apprentice Handicap
Spotlight: Made the breakthrough at Chelmsford in December and nearly doubled up at Lingfield (also 7f, AW) that month; way off that when last seen in January though and would become of interest only with market support on this comeback.

RaceName: Burwell Apprentice Handicap
OwnerName: Lady Emma Oshea And A Carson
Spotlight: Veteran who gained a first win on turf in Yarmouth classified event (6f, good to firm) three weeks ago; work to do back in a handicap but this isn't the deepest of contests.

RaceName: Always Trying Racing Syndicate Handicap
Spotlight: Took this prize 12 months ago and she's back in excellent form of late, scoring at Carlisle (1m) before third of seven over C&D 26 days ago; merits serious consideration in her follow-up bid.

RaceName: Always Trying Racing Syndicate Handicap
Spotlight: On a losing run and he looked rusty after eight months off when tenth of 14 in 1m2f handicap at York on his return 20 days ago; this C&D winner can take a step forward here.

RaceName: Always Trying Racing Syndicate Handicap
Spotlight: A dual C&D winner in 2025; yet to hit top form this season but took a step back in the right direction with fifth of nine over C&D ten days ago; blinkers replace usual cheekpieces here and not out of things.

RaceName: Always Trying Racing Syndicate Handicap
Spotlight: A fair 1m maiden for Archie Watson at three but not seen since a below-par eighth at Newcastle (1m) in October; has since changed hands for 4,000gns and needs to hit the ground running for her new yard after nine months off.

RaceName: Always Trying Racing Syndicate Handicap
Spotlight: Placed three times (7f/1m) for Roger Varian in 2025 but yet to show that form in a trio of runs for her current yard, fourth of eight in 1m2f handicap at Ripon 30 days ago; needs to take a step forward.

RaceName: Always Trying Racing Syndicate Handicap
Spotlight: Modest maiden who has failed to kick on since sent handicapping, just fifth of eight at Ripon (1m2f) last month; visor is reached for now.

RaceName: Always Trying Racing Syndicate Handicap
Spotlight: Gained a breakthrough win at Wolverhampton (8.5f) in December but not seen since going too freely when last of five in 1m2f AW handicap at Lingfield in January; yard is going very well though so can't be ruled out back in trip here.

RaceName: Always Trying Racing Syndicate Handicap
Spotlight: Consistent sort who bagged a deserved first victory at Ayr (1m) in June and not disgraced with sixth of ten there (1m) 11 days ago; needs considering.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Won at Ayr (1m, good to firm) in May and she's twice gone close here since, over C&D and at 1m3f; features prominently in calculations.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: 80-1 winner here (1m1f, good to firm) in May on her reappearance and second start; tailed off on handicap debut at Redcar (1m2f, good) since and needs to prove her course win was no fluke, but she's not written off.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Encouraging debut for Jessica Harrington at Punchestown (1m, good to yielding) last September; however, her form went the wrong way across her next three starts and she was sold for 2,200gns last month; needs to turn things around on her stable debut.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Six wins last September-December (including C&D) and she's gone very close to adding to that tally on her last two outings, beaten just a neck over C&D (good) last Saturday despite being badly hampered; lots to like.

RaceName: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: 3yo who has finished second or third on all six starts, most recently runner-up at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) 11 days ago in first-time blinkers (retained); she'll be fine back on turf and can again be in the shake-up.
